attributes.description: Alerts when any ranges have fewer replicas than the replication target (`ranges_underreplicated > 0`). The cluster is still serving traffic but has lost redundancy — further failures could cause unavailability.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] Under-replicated ranges detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB under-replicated ranges

### What fired
`ranges_underreplicated` on at least one store exceeded 0 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
An under-replicated range is still serving traffic but has fewer replicas than the replication factor specifies. The cluster has lost its safety margin: one more replica failure in the affected range(s) can cause unavailability.

### Triage
1. Check `liveness_livenodes`. Are any nodes down or decommissioning?
2. Inspect `ranges_underreplicated` by `attributes.node_id` / `attributes.store` to see whether the issue is concentrated or spread.
3. Look at `rebalancing_range_rebalances` — if up-replication is in flight, the metric will drain on its own.
4. Check available capacity on surviving stores (`capacity_used / capacity`); full stores cannot host new replicas.

### Remediation
- If a node is down, bring it back or replace it.
- If capacity is full, add nodes; CockroachDB will up-replicate automatically once capacity exists.
- Check zone configs haven't accidentally been set to a replication factor exceeding the number of failure domains.

### Tuning
- The 10-minute window filters brief rebalancing dips. Extend to 15m+ if your cluster frequently rebalances.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND ranges_underreplicated IS NOT NULL
  AND ranges_underreplicated > 0
// Store-level metric
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NOT NULL
| STATS underreplicated = MAX(ranges_underreplicated) BY attributes.node_id, attributes.store, host.name
| SORT underreplicated DESC
